target.title: docs: clarify OpenClaw vs Codex code mode
target.body:
## Summary

- Closes #83390.
- Adds a side-by-side comparison table for OpenClaw code mode vs Codex Code Mode in `docs/reference/code-mode.md`.
- Adds a reverse link from `docs/plugins/codex-harness.md` so Codex harness readers can find the comparison anchor.
- Corrects Codex Code Mode wording from stale `exec.command` shell-command phrasing to upstream-backed raw JavaScript/TypeScript source through `exec`.
- No runtime, config, provider, or migration behavior changes.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:**
Readers were unclear how OpenClaw native code mode differs from Codex Code Mode (defaults, runtime owner, policy path, and `exec` contract). This docs-only patch adds the comparison table and cross-link without changing runtime behavior.

**Real environment tested:**
OpenClaw repo checkout on Linux x86_64, Node 22, patched branch `docs/issue-83390-code-mode-clarity` at `c477c0ba18f`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
pnpm docs:list | rg "code-mode|codex-harness"
pnpm docs:check-mdx
pnpm docs:check-links
git diff --check docs/reference/code-mode.md docs/plugins/codex-harness.md
rg -n "OpenClaw code mode vs Codex Code Mode|raw JavaScript or TypeScript" docs/reference/code-mode.md docs/plugins/codex-harness.md
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Terminal capture from the patched branch showing docs validation and the new comparison content present:

```text
$ pnpm docs:list | rg "code-mode|codex-harness"
plugins/codex-harness.md - Run OpenClaw embedded agent turns through the bundled Codex app-server harness
reference/code-mode.md - OpenClaw code mode: an opt-in exec/wait tool surface backed by QuickJS-WASI and a hidden run-scoped tool catalog

$ pnpm docs:check-mdx
$ node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md
Docs MDX check passed (692 files, 4709ms).

$ pnpm docs:check-links
$ node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs
checked_internal_links=4468
broken_links=0

$ git diff --check docs/reference/code-mode.md docs/plugins/codex-harness.md
(no output)

$ rg -n "OpenClaw code mode vs Codex Code Mode|raw JavaScript or TypeScript" docs/reference/code-mode.md docs/plugins/codex-harness.md
docs/reference/code-mode.md:23:  where the model writes raw JavaScript or TypeScript source through an `exec` contract.
docs/reference/code-mode.md:33:## OpenClaw code mode vs Codex Code Mode
docs/plugins/codex-harness.md:44:[OpenClaw code mode vs Codex Code Mode](/reference/code-mode#openclaw-code-mode-vs-codex-code-mode).
```

**Observed result after fix:**
- Both changed docs remain indexed by `pnpm docs:list`.
- MDX validation passes for the full docs tree (692 files).
- Internal link audit reports `broken_links=0`, including the new cross-link to `#openclaw-code-mode-vs-codex-code-mode`.
- The comparison heading, corrected Codex `exec` wording, and reverse link are present at the expected lines.

**What was not tested:**
Live Mintlify preview on docs.openclaw.ai; runtime Codex app-server or OpenClaw code-mode execution paths (docs-only change).

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm docs:list | rg "code-mode|codex-harness"`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm docs:check-links`
- `git diff --check docs/reference/code-mode.md docs/plugins/codex-harness.md`
- `rg -n "OpenClaw code mode vs Codex Code Mode|raw JavaScript or TypeScript" docs/reference/code-mode.md docs/plugins/codex-harness.md`

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? No
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No
Highest-risk area: Docs wording only; incorrect comparison text could mislead readers.
Mitigation: Wording verified against current `src/agents/code-mode.ts`, `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts`, and upstream Codex execute handler source cited in review.

## Current review state

Next action: await maintainer merge after CI + ClawSweeper refresh on head `c477c0ba18f`.
Waiting on: `proof: sufficient` and final maintainer review.
Bot comments addressed: rebased onto current `main`, refreshed Real behavior proof with terminal capture, corrected stale Codex `exec.command` wording.

Closes #83390.
target.author: pick-cat
